content.full_text:
June 20, 1990

Mr. John Wallace  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Wallace:

This letter is to ask that you confirm in writing the advice that you gave me in our telephone conversation of June 19, 1990. As I explained to you, I am a Planning Commissioner with the City of Atascadero who recently ran unsuccessfully for City Council. I stepped down from the commission (April 3, 1990) when it considered Tentative Parcel Map 02-89, because I had received a 300 dollar campaign contribution from a couple that live adjacent to the property.

The Commission recommended denial of the Tentative Map and the City Council continued the item until after the election. The new City Council will hear the item next Tuesday (June 26, 1990).

My question was "May I speak before the City Council on this matter?". Your advice was that I can, provided that I speak as a private citizen.

Should you change your mind please contact me at once. My phone number is 805 466 2946.

Thank you.

Sincerely yours,

George Luna  
George Luna  
10600 San Marcos  
Atascadero, Ca.  
93422
